name: Sundaresh Menon
current_positions: President of the Court of Appeal
Chairman of the Advisory Board for SMU School of Law
Deputy Chairman of the Singapore International Arbitration Centre
Member of the Senate of the Singapore Academy of Law

work_experience.position: Founding Partner (co-founded with Wong Meng Meng and Alvin Yeo)
Partner, Head of Projects & Infrastructure Group
Senior Counsel (appointed 5 January 2008), Managing Partner (from August 2009)
Justice of the Court of Appeal

person_relationships.description: Both served in the judiciary; Menon as CJ and Nair as Justice of the Court of Appeal under Menon's leadership; also connected through AG's Chambers (Menon as AG, Nair as DAG)
Preceded Menon as Chief Justice; Menon succeeded Chan on 6 November 2012

political_stances.stance_content: Advocated for the establishment of the Singapore International Commercial Court (SICC) to internationalise Singapore law and strengthen cross-border dispute resolution
Championed court reforms in legal education, court processes, and professional ethics, including the adoption of technology in the judiciary
Delivered notable judgment in Tan Seng Kee v Attorney-General (Section 377A challenge), which was criticised by former CJ Chan Sek Keong in an extra-judicial article

major_achievements.achievement: Co-founded WongPartnership, now one of the largest law firms in Singapore
Appointed Senior Counsel (SC), the highest professional recognition for lawyers in Singapore
Appointed as the 4th Chief Justice of Singapore; first CJ born in Singapore; appointed by President Tony Tan
Established the Singapore International Commercial Court (SICC), positioning Singapore as a leading venue for international commercial dispute resolution
Conferred an honorary degree by NUS in recognition of his contributions to law

biography_summary: Sundaresh Menon SC (born 26 February 1962) is a Singaporean lawyer and judge who has served as the fourth Chief Justice of Singapore since 2012. Born in Singapore, he graduated from the National University of Singapore with a First Class Honours LLB in 1986 and obtained an LLM from Harvard Law School in 1991. He began his career at Shook Lin & Bok (1987-1990), co-founded WongPartnership (1992-1995) with Wong Meng Meng and Alvin Yeo, then served as partner at Rajah & Tann and Jones Day. He was appointed Judicial Commissioner (2006-2007), then returned to practice as Senior Counsel and managing partner of Rajah & Tann. He served as Attorney-General (2010-2012) before being appointed Chief Justice by President Tony Tan on 6 November 2012. He is the first Singapore-born Chief Justice. He established the Singapore International Commercial Court (SICC) in 2015 and has overseen significant reforms in legal education, court technology, and judicial cooperation across Asia. Notable judgments include Tan Seng Kee v AG (Section 377A challenge) and Nagaenthran v Public Prosecutor (death penalty case).

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with co-founder relationships (Wong Meng Meng, Alvin Yeo), notable judgments (Tan Seng Kee, Nagaenthran), predecessor relationship with Chan Sek Keong, and expanded political stances including 377A judgment. No family/spouse information available in public sources. NUS honorary degree added.
